title: Syzygium oblanceolatum
taxonomy: Syzygium oblanceolatum is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae. It belongs to the genus Syzygium, which is a group of plants commonly known as water apples or wax apples. The family Myrtaceae is a large and diverse group of flowering plants, including various species of trees, shrubs, and herbaceous plants. Syzygium oblanceolatum is native to Australia, where it can be found in the tropical and subtropical regions. The plant is often classified based on its botanical characteristics, such as its leaf shape and size, and its fruiting habits.
